soap:
RE-REVIEW · lance-format/lance#7245 — fix(python): use mimalloc as global allocator to fix RSS leak
reviewed 1b8d31d9 → 686c79c1  (3 commits, 4 files)
lane: re_review · viewer: wjones127 · prior review state: CHANGES_REQUESTED @ 1b8d31d9

S — SUBJECTIVE (what changed since I last looked)
  3 commits landed on the delta 1b8d31d9...686c79c1:
    587bba19  style: add license header to benchmark_allocators.py
    6918b01b  fix(python): enable mimalloc local_dynamic_tls and update lockfile
    686c79c1  fix(python): make mimalloc and jemalloc allocators mutually exclusive
  Net effect: the previously-flagged static-TLS landmine is addressed, and the
  mimalloc/jemalloc global_allocator selection is hardened against double
  registration. The benchmark script the author was asked NOT to check in is
  still present (license header added instead of removal).

O — OBJECTIVE (ground truth from the patch + head files)
  Files in delta:
    python/Cargo.lock            +40/-0   (lockfile, follows mimalloc feature)
    python/Cargo.toml            +6/-1
    python/benchmark_allocators.py +2/-0
    python/src/lib.rs            +3/-1

  Cargo.toml [features] @ head:
    default   = ["mimalloc"]
    mimalloc  = ["dep:mimalloc"]
    jemalloc  = ["dep:tikv-jemallocator"]
    mimalloc dep now built with features = ["local_dynamic_tls"]

  lib.rs @ head:
    #[cfg(all(feature = "mimalloc", not(feature = "jemalloc")))]
    #[global_allocator] static GLOBAL: mimalloc::MiMalloc = ...;
    #[cfg(all(feature = "jemalloc", not(target_os = "windows")))]
    #[global_allocator] static GLOBAL: tikv_jemallocator::Jemalloc = ...;

A — ASSESSMENT

  CONDITIONS LEDGER (0/0 met)
    No closed-ended issue(blocking) conditions were reconstructed from the prior
    review. My earlier review was CHANGES_REQUESTED but carried no blocking
    inline ledger items — only one non-blocking suggestion (below). Section A is
    therefore empty by construction, not by oversight.

  FRESH-EYES DELTA
    1. local_dynamic_tls fix — CORRECT.
       mimalloc with default-features off previously used the initial-exec TLS
       model; dlopen'ing the extension could exhaust glibc's static-TLS surplus
       ("cannot allocate memory in static TLS block"). Switching to
       local_dynamic_tls is the standard remedy and the comment explains the WHY.
       → python/Cargo.toml hunk @ line 62
    2. mutual-exclusivity fix — CORRECT, verified against the cfg matrix.
       mimalloc gate gained `not(feature = "jemalloc")`. Across the feature
       matrix on non-Windows: jemalloc-only → jemalloc; mimalloc-only → mimalloc;
       both → jemalloc wins (matches the "jemalloc takes precedence" comment);
       no target now registers two #[global_allocator] statics, which would have
       been a hard compile error. The previous code (plain `cfg(feature =
       "mimalloc")`) would double-register whenever both features were on.
       → python/src/lib.rs hunk @ lines 22-30
       Benign residual (non-blocking): Windows + both features enabled →
       mimalloc excluded by jemalloc-feature, jemalloc excluded by target_os →
       zero global_allocator, silent fall back to the system allocator. Not a
       regression (jemalloc was already Windows-gated) and not a compile error;
       noting for completeness, not blocking.
    3. license header on benchmark_allocators.py — fine in isolation, but it
       doubles down on checking the script in. My prior (non-blocking)
       suggestion was to NOT commit it and instead run the full benchmark suite
       across main / --feature mimalloc / --feature jemalloc and report numbers.
       That suggestion is unaddressed. It was non-blocking then and stays
       non-blocking now — re-raise as a nit, do not gate merge on it.

    No new bad behavior introduced in the changed regions. The lockfile delta is
    consistent with the mimalloc feature change.

P — PLAN / RECOMMENDATION: approve

  The two substantive changes since my last review (TLS model + allocator
  mutual exclusion) are both correct and verified against the head source, not
  just the author's claims. There are zero outstanding blocking conditions. The
  only open item is a non-blocking suggestion about not vendoring the benchmark
  script, which should not gate merge.

  DRAFT non-blocking comments to carry forward (do not post here):
    nit(benchmark_allocators.py): prefer not checking this script in; if kept,
      attach a one-time benchmark table (main vs --feature mimalloc vs
      --feature jemalloc, RSS over the existing python/python/benchmarks suite)
      to the PR description so the RSS-leak claim is reproducible.

  confidence: high · what would make this wrong: a third #[global_allocator]
  registration (e.g. snmalloc) elsewhere in python/src that I did not see, or a
  CI/build target where the Windows+both-features zero-allocator gap actually
  matters. Neither appears in the delta.
